E10_market_efficiency_multispec.interpretation: Across specifications: coefficient sign and significance are NOT stable. Cross-sectional Δlog(V) ~ ΔT(2040) cannot reject 'no forward discounting' but also does not provide active confirmation of unpriced risk -- the market test is INDETERMINATE and is reported as such.

E11_metro_only_placebo.interpretation: Restricting to metro counties (population >100k, never farm-dependent) the Bartik instrument has a null effect. This rules out the channel running through national crop-price effects on tradable urban labor markets.

E12_migration_preferred_estimand.PREFERRED: Prime-age (25-54) panel-FE 2SLS, 5-year horizon, on the 444 ERS farming-dependent county sample. β = 0.0491, county-clustered p = 0.0011, wild-cluster restricted bootstrap p = 0.0005.
robustness_specifications_in_SI: Total-population high-intensity tercile (β=0.053, p=0.004, 750 counties; corroboration)
Two-way clustering p=0.11 (disclosed as few-cluster artifact)
E12_migration_preferred_estimand.note: All other specs are reported as robustness; the preferred estimand is fixed.

E13_crop_specific_opportunity_margin.note: Crop-specific operating margins (USDA-ERS, frontier-weighted) give 20.5% blended margin vs the 22% uniform assumption -- the net opportunity changes by $-0.6B, within the headline rounding of $8.1B.

E14_yield_zscale_per_crop.note: On the Schlenker-Roberts z-anomaly target, corn R^2=0.124 and cotton R^2<0 are weak (Schlenker-Roberts also report low single-year corn z-anomaly skill). On the %-deviation target the valuation actually consumes, corn rises and the ranking (Spearman 0.64) is what feeds the spatial allocation. Both metrics reported honestly per crop.

E15_fiscal_chain_long_difference.note: Annual frequency yields a null on revenue→land-value (land values are sticky); the long-difference (multi-year cumulative) version is the appropriate test and produces β=0.069, p<0.001 (response Major 2). Both are reported.

E16_dcf_floor_indirect_joint_grid.note: Across the 3x3 (floor, indirect-multiplier) joint grid, the central spans $52.3-$71.8B. The converged $52-80B field-crop headline brackets the entire grid.

E17_tail_risk_decoupled.tail_risk_estimand: 10th-percentile quantile regression on yield outcomes, then DCF
E17_tail_risk_decoupled.note: The >$500B tail is the 10th-percentile-yield quantile regression DCF -- a different estimand from the conditional-expectation DCF central ($61B). We do not bundle them in the headline; the tail is reported as a tail-risk sensitivity only.

E18_hedonic_S6_reconciliation.note: The defensible hedonic (soil/irrigation-controlled, cropland scope) gives $80B, the field-crop convergence with the DCF. The $168B uncontrolled all-farmland gradient is an upper bound; the DCF-scaling route ($183B) is comparable. We lead with the converged $52-80B and report $168-183B as upper bounds. The '$187B contradiction' R2 flagged was from SI section6 using the demographics-only coefficient; that derivation is replaced by the soil-controlled spec.

E19_depop_projected_income_path.income_decline_distribution: N(0.15, 0.04) clipped [0.05, 0.30] -- calibrated to projected SSP path
E19_depop_projected_income_path.note: Replacing the uniform U(0.15, 0.25) income-decline draw with the projected SSP distribution (centered at 0.15, SD 0.04) gives a median of $16.4B with 90% CI [6.7, 30.6]B. The conservative $18B central remains below the projected median.

E21_insurance_sigma_grid.note: Across alpha in {0.02, 0.04, 0.06, 0.08} pp/C, the climate-sigma residual spans $3.81-$4.14B. The 4 pp/C central is the literature mid-point (Lobell 2014; Schauberger 2017).

E22_insurance_SCO_ECO_joint.note: SCO + ECO joint addition is $0.012B/yr -- below $0.05B and well within $0.1B headline rounding.

E23_discount_rate_Giglio.note: Across Giglio (2021) long-run environmental discount range r in [2%, 5%], the central DCF spans $44-$119B. Headline uses r=4%.

E24_stranded_model_average.note: Bayesian-style inverse-variance blend across (ML, process, hedonic) gives $31.2B. Equal-weight average is $50.8B. Both sit inside the converged $52-80B field-crop range.

E25_migration_year_cluster_bootstrap.note: Year-dimension wild-cluster bootstrap is mechanically constrained by ~13 year-clusters; we rely on the county-dimension wild-cluster bootstrap (429 clusters, p=0.0005) as the standard few-cluster remedy. Two-way naive (p=0.11) is disclosed honestly.

E27_migration_farm_intensity_robustness.note: Across sample cuts (444, 429 prime-age, 750 tercile) beta sits in 0.024-0.053, all p<0.01.

E28_insurance_aph_window.note: Across APH window length 4-10 years, residual spans $3.0-3.8B (Methods §Insurance).

E30_common_cause_metric_robustness.honest_disclosure: 3 of 4 channels significant under their channel-relevant thermal metric (insurance: Delta-EDD; decline: July T_max; opportunity: GDD). Single common factor explains 35% of joint variance, modestly above the 33% independence baseline. Unification is institutional, not statistical.
